summary: Clear sound‑board capture with a superb acoustic set and soaring New Speedway Boogie, highlighted by an epic Cryptical → Drums → The Other One sequence.
review: This June 7 1970 Fillmore West show shines thanks to a tight acoustic first set and a second‑set that bursts through the Cryptical/Drums/Other One suite, with New Speedway Boogie and Good Lovin' jams standing out. The SBD source is generally clear but suffers occasional bass heaviness, tape warble and a glitch in Cumberland Blues. Overall the performance outweighs the minor sonic flaws.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 20 reviews overwhelmingly praise the musical performance while noting only modest recording imperfections; the combined weighting yields a strong rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally clear and well‑balanced SBD, but listeners report heavy bass, occasional tape warble/hissing, and a splice glitch in Cumberland Blues.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A relaxed acoustic first set leads into a jam‑heavy second set where the Cryptical‑Drums‑Other One suite provides superb continuity; transitions are praised even when individual songs (e.g., Sugar Magnolia) stumble.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Voice on New Speedway Boogie highlighted as inspired, occasional vocal strain noted; also praised for pedal‑steel work during acoustic numbers.
band_member_comments.Phil: Some ragged playing and off‑key harmonies mentioned (Silver Threads), otherwise no major commentary.
band_member_comments.Bob: Weir’s lead vocals on Cosmic Charlie called out positively; his banter with the crowd remembered fondly.
band_member_comments.Drums: Quiet, atmospheric drum interludes between Cryptical and The Other One praised; overall drum work described as “fabulous” and “nice build‑up.”
